content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Apr 8, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development activity on April 8, 2026, focused on expanding the ecosystem's cross-chain capabilities and advancing community discussions regarding multi-agent coordination and identity verification.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*   **Cross-Chain Integration**
    *   Initiated the addition of a new plugin to facilitate cross-chain swaps across 10+ chains via the `@mangoswap/elizaos-plugin` integration ([#336](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/336)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#336](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/336): feat: add @mangoswap/elizaos-plugin — cross-chain swaps for 10+ chains.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   **[#6514](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6514) Inter-Agent Coordination**
    *   Community discussion continues regarding cross-instance communication and agent identity.
    *   Latest contributions emphasize the importance of "behavioral history" alongside wallet-based credentials. 
    *   Proposed solutions include using append-only on-chain attestation sequences (e.g., WTRMRK) to verify the longitudinal reliability and activity depth of peer agents, helping to solve cold-start trust problems when agents interact for the first time.
    *   Participants highlighted that persistent UIDs and keypairs, independent of deployment endpoints, are essential for maintaining identity across host migrations or restarts.
